div.head: Mr. Fish to General Schenck
div.p: Sir: Referring to my No. 3 of the 19th ultimo, in relation to the pending negotiations of a consular convention with the British government, I will thank you to propose the adoption of an article providing for the administration of oaths by the consuls of either country residing in the other. This you will do at such time and in such manner as may, in your judgment, be most opportune and appropriate. This is especially to be desired by this Government, as consuls are required, by existing Treasury regulations, to see that all invoices verified by them are supported by the oath of the party.
